title: rosa × dumetorum subvar. acanthina
intro: Rosa × dumetorum subvar. acanthina, commonly known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of rose plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating a variety of health issues. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum subvar. acanthina include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with colds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as promoting skin and hair health. Rosa × dumetorum subvar. acanthina can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
